mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of months where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is persistently high and the Federal Reserve responds by increasing the federal funds rate, it signals a restrictive monetary policy that dampens economic activity and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS increased by 50bps in 6 months
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in > 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth is above 3% and FEDFUNDS has increased by at least 50bps in the last 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle expansion, robust growth in industrial production indicates increasing manufacturing activity and business investment, which typically precedes strong corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: In identified early-cycle expansion periods, INDPRO month-over-month growth does not correlate positively with SPX 5d returns (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding a key psychological threshold of 1 million units per month indicates strong builder confidence and underlying economic health, suggesting positive spillover effects into broader consumption and S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts above 1M units often seen as healthy.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in > 50% of months when HOUST is above 1000k units.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by nonfarm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which supports consumer spending and corporate revenues, ultimately driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the medium 60-day term.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is maintained at extremely low levels (below 0.25%), it signifies a highly accommodative monetary policy aimed at stimulating economic growth, which typically supports equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the low FEDFUNDS rate.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust GDP growth and strong retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength and consumer confidence, signaling a healthy environment for corporate profits and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: When both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive year-over-year growth, SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 45% of observations.
